Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-21-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: CROSS-COMPLAINANT 653 28TH STREET, LLC's Motion For Leave To File A Second Amended Cross-Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant and Cross-Complainant 653 28th Street, LLC's motion for leave to file a second amended cross-complaint is granted. Cross-Defendants do not show that Cross-Complainant has unreasonably delayed in seeking leave to amend or that they would be prejudiced by allowing the amendment. To the extent that they wish to challenge the viability of the proposed new cause of action under Bus. & Prof. Code section 7160, they may do so by filing an appropriate motion. (See California Casualty Gen. Ins. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].)

Order signed.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Further Hearing On: Defendant 653 28th Street, Llc'S Motion To Stay State Court Proceedings In Their Entirety Pending The Outcome Of Arbitration.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Consistent with the Court's July 19, 2019 order granting reconsideration, Defendant 653 28th Street, LLC's motion to stay this litigation pending the completion of the pending contractual arbitration between it and Plaintiff Arcon Construction Corporation is granted.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which vacates its prior order and repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Motion And Notice Of Hearing To Stay State Court Proceedings In Their Entirety Pending The Outcome Of Arbitration
Off Calendar Jul-18-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
George Wolff, Esq. 415-788-1881 x222; Michael Squeri, Esq. 415-788-1881 x232 of the Law Office of George William Wolff for Plaintiff.
Jan Gruen, Esq. 925-943-6570 of Horner Law Group PC for Defendant 653 28TH STREET LLC.
Jennifer Letulle, Esq. 415-986-5900 of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani LLP for Defendant John Lum Architecture Inc. and John git Hoong Lum through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views and considerations. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT 653 28TH STREET LLC's Motion To Stay State Court Proceedings In Their Entirety Pending The Outcome Of Arbitration.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling and adds without Prejudice. Defendant 653 28th Street, LLC's motion to stay is denied without Prejudice. Contrary to Defendant's contention, Code of Civil Procedure Section 1281.4 does not mandate a stay of the litigation. Section 1281.4 applies only "[i]f a court of competent jurisdiction . . . has ordered arbitration of a controversy which is an issue involved in an action or proceeding pending before a court of this State," or if "an application has been made to a court of competent jurisdiction . . . for an order to arbitrate" such a controversy. Thus, the statutory language "requires a court to grant a party's motion to stay when the court has ordered arbitration of an issue in the action." (Thomas v.Westlake (2012) 204 Cal.App.4th 605, 620 (emphasis added).) Here, no such court order was sought or entered. Further, the contractual arbitration is currently scheduled for December 2019, and the first amended complaint in this action was only recently filed and served. Accordingly, on the current record, the Court does not believe there is a significant risk of inconsistent rulings; accordingly, Plaintiff Arcon Construction Corporation's request pursuant to Section 1281.2 to stay the arbitration pending the outcome of this court proceeding is also denied without Prejudice.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
